supply_list.quote_context: For every visit, the CHW completes the Sick Child Recording Form.
Dissolve the zinc tablet (or half tablet) in breast milk or clean water on a spoon.
Count the child's breaths for one full minute while the child is calm and quiet.
Use the MUAC (Mid-Upper Arm Circumference) strap on children age 6 months up to 5 years.
Give ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts) and zinc supplement.
Give ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts) and zinc supplement.
If breathing is normal and child is improving, complete the full 5-day course of amoxicillin.
Do a Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) for malaria before treating.
2 months up to 3 years 1 tablet AL Twice daily 3 days / 6 tablets
3 years up to 5 years 2 tablets AL Twice daily 3 days / 12 tablets

variables.span: 3 or more loose or watery stools in 24 hours

variables.span: Wrap the strap around the child's left upper arm, midway between shoulder and elbow
variables.quote_context: Wrap the strap around the child's left upper arm, midway between shoulder and elbow. Read the colour:
Wrap the strap around the child's left upper arm, midway between shoulder and elbow. Read the colour:
Also check for swelling of both feet (oedema). Press thumbs gently on the top of each foot for 3 seconds.

variables.span: Press thumbs gently on the top of each foot for 3 seconds
variables.quote_context: Press thumbs gently on the top of each foot for 3 seconds. If dents remain on BOTH feet, the child has severe malnutrition.
5.1 Diarrhoea (less than 14 days, no blood, no danger sign)
After 3 days: Is the child still coughing? Count breaths again.

modules.span: This is a danger sign indicating severe pneumonia
modules.quote_context: This is a danger sign indicating severe pneumonia. The child must be calm; do not assess while crying or breastfeeding.
Oral amoxicillin dosage (250 mg tablets):

Cough
Ask: Does the child have a cough?

Fever
Ask: Does the child have fever now or in the last 3 days?

Diarrhoea
Diarrhoea is 3 or more loose or watery stools in 24 hours.

Fast breathing
Count the child's breaths for one full minute while the child is calm and quiet.

Convulsions
A convulsion during the current illness is a danger sign.

Chest indrawing
Look at the lower chest wall when the child breathes IN.

Vomiting everything
A child who vomits everything and cannot hold anything down has a danger sign.
